title: India FY 2024-25 New Tax Regime (simplified)
text: Standard deduction under new regime is typically ₹75,000 for salaried. Slabs are progressive from 0% up to 30% above ₹15 lakh taxable income. Old vs new regime choice depends on deductions like 80C, HRA, home loan interest. Always compare both with actual numbers.

title: Emergency fund guideline
text: Salaried households often target 3-6 months of essential expenses in liquid assets (sweep FD, liquid funds). Self-employed may need 6-12 months. Credit card debt should not replace emergency savings.

title: SIP and compounding
text: SIP (Systematic Investment Plan) averages purchase price over time (rupee cost averaging). Long-term equity returns are uncertain; use realistic assumptions (e.g. 10-12% illustrative) only for planning, not guarantees.

title: Term insurance (India context)
text: Human Life Value heuristic: often 10-15x annual income as sum assured starting point, adjusted for liabilities and goals. Pure term plans are for protection; avoid mixing with investment unless you understand costs.
